Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Service Of Summons; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support; Declaration
Off Calendar Jan-04-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT RON WHITEHEAD's Motion To Quash Service Of Summons for Lack of Jurisdiction.

Defendant Ron Whitehead, Jr.'s motion to quash is GRANTED. It is Bank of the West's burden to establish sufficient minimum contacts for the court to exercise jurisdiction over Whitehead. (See Mihlon v. Sup. Crt. (1985) 169 Cal.App.3d 703, 710.) For specific jurisdiction, "the crucial inquiry concerns the character of defendant's activity in the forum, whether the cause of action arises out of or has a substantial connection with that activity, and upon the balancing of the convenience of the parties and the interests of the state in assuming jurisdiction." (Vons Companies, Inc. v. Seabest Foods, Inc., (1996) 14 Cal.4th 434, 448 (italics in original).)

In this case, Bank of the West's citation to the complaint and the declaration of counsel Cefalu is insufficient to demonstrate jurisdiction. The underlying transaction has no nexus to California. Whitehead resides in Texas, he travelled to Tennessee to discuss purchase/lease of the PDS printer, and entered into a financing agreement with Connext (an Indiana entity) that contemplated that the printer would be shipped to Texas. (Whitehead Dec., pars. 1-7.) Bank of the West cannot manufacture jurisdiction by subsequently becoming an assignee of Connext. Whitehead's contacts with California (attending trade shows, selling denim in California) are irrelevant to the instant action. Those contacts have no relationship to the claims that form the basis of this action.

The court exercises its discretion and denies Bank of the West's request for jurisdictional discovery. (See HealthMarkets, Inc. v. Sup. Crt. (2009) 171 Cal.App.4th 1160, 1173.) Bank of the West does not highlight what discovery it would like to conduct or what relevant facts it hopes to unearth to establish jurisdiction. As stated, the fact that Whitehead may engage in unrelated business transactions in California over the internet is irrelevant to the specific jurisdiction analysis.

Counsel for Whitehead, Jr. to submit a proposed order that is verbatim with the Court's ruling in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/RBU)
